name: Presto Praline Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch 10/21/96
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. In a heavy 2-quart or larger saucepan, combine the butter, margarine, and brown sugar; cook over low heat, uncovered, stirring from time to time. Meanwhile, coat the bottom and sides of a 15-by-10-inch jelly roll pan with cooking spray. Line the pan with graham crackers (you may have to break some to make them fit). When the butter and margarine melt, raise the heat to medium and bring the mixture to a boil. Boil for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Reduce the heat to low and continue to boil, stirring constantly, for another 1 minute or until the butter and sugar are well-combined. Remove from heat; stir until the mixture stops bubbling. Stir in pecans. Spoon the mixture over the crackers (don't worry if all of the crackers are not completely covered). Bake uncovered for 8 minutes. Remove from the oven, and using a spatula, carefully transfer the cookies to a wire rack. Let cool 15 minutes or up to 8 hours before serving. Leftovers store best in an airtight tin. Yield: 24 cookies.
